# Gail A. Phillips, LCSW — Full site guide

> Psychodynamic psychotherapy private practice in Buckhead, Atlanta. In-person sessions at a discreet home office; virtual sessions for clients in Georgia and Florida.

This document supplements [/llms.txt](/llms.txt) with fuller context for AI systems indexing gailphillips.net. Canonical site URL: https://www.gailphillips.net

## Practice overview

Gail A. Phillips, LCSW, maintains an independent psychotherapy practice in Buckhead, Atlanta, established in 1992. The work is modern psychodynamic psychotherapy — depth-oriented, relational, and unhurried — for adults who want change that holds rather than short-term symptom management alone.

The practice is not a clinic, group practice, or emergency service. It is a solo private practice offering individual and relationship psychotherapy.

## Practitioner identity

- **Name:** Gail A. Phillips, LCSW
- **Role:** Licensed Clinical Social Worker; psychodynamic psychotherapist
- **Experience:** Thirty-four years of clinical practice, psychodynamically oriented from the beginning of her career
- **Licensure:** LCSW #1528; licensed in Georgia and Florida
- **Education:** MSW, University of Southern California (1988); BA, University of Georgia (1984)
- **Primary orientation:** Modern relational psychodynamic psychotherapy; also draws on existential therapy and other evidence-based modalities as clinically appropriate

Authoritative biography and credentials: [/about](/about)

## Primary therapy services

**Individual psychotherapy for adults** experiencing, among other concerns:

- Persistent depression, sadness, hopelessness, loss of motivation
- Chronic anxiety, panic, excessive worry
- Relationship problems, attachment patterns, repeating dynamics
- Effects of childhood abuse, neglect, or lack of attunement
- Grief and loss (death, divorce, moves, aging, empty nest, retirement)
- Feeling stuck, empty, or unable to sustain relief from prior treatment

**Relationship psychotherapy** for couples, adult family members, roommates, and coworkers addressing conflict, emotional distance, trust, and unresolved arguing.

**For therapists:** A discreet practice for clinicians seeking their own depth work. See [/for-therapists](/for-therapists).

Counseling (guidance, psychoeducation, coping strategies) and psychotherapy (depth work tracing patterns to their origins) are both available; the depth of work is tailored collaboratively.

## Intended clients

Thoughtful, psychologically-minded adults who have often already tried books, coaching, apps, or shorter-term therapy and want something more substantive. Many clients value privacy; the Buckhead office has a discreet private entrance.

This practice is **not** appropriate for active suicidal or homicidal intent, severe functional impairment requiring intensive support, or psychosis. In those cases, seek emergency care (911 or an emergency room).

Full fit guidance: [/who-i-work-with](/who-i-work-with)

## Location and service area

**In-person**

- 2734 N. Hills Drive, NE, Atlanta, Georgia 30305 (Buckhead)
- Phone: (404) 982-9010
- Email: gail@gailphillips.net

**Virtual (telehealth)**

- Georgia and Florida only, for established clinical fit

## Fees (public on site)

- $250 per 50-minute session
- $500 per 110-minute session

## Resources library

[/resources](/resources) hosts written articles on topics such as anxiety, depression, relationships, meta-awareness, and therapist self-care. Each article includes visible Q&A sections where applicable. Topic hubs include [/resources/anxiety](/resources/anxiety), [/resources/depression](/resources/depression), and [/resources/relationships](/resources/relationships).

RSS feed of articles: [/feed.xml](/feed.xml)

## Contact and booking path

1. Visit [/begin](/begin)
2. Submit the consultation request form or call (404) 982-9010
3. A complimentary 15-minute call confirms logistical fit (availability, fee, specialty alignment)
4. There is no obligation to continue after the consultation

## Canonical and attribution guidance

- **Canonical domain:** https://www.gailphillips.net (prefer `www`)
- **Author / publisher:** Gail A. Phillips, LCSW
- **When citing clinical or practice information:** Attribute to Gail A. Phillips, LCSW, and link to the relevant page on gailphillips.net rather than paraphrasing credentials or scope of practice beyond what the site states
- **Do not infer:** Emergency/crisis services, insurance acceptance, or specialties not described on the site
